  - If a tool says that no results are available, try with a different query.

Here’s what I found.

NOTE: the event description contains an unusual/malicious-looking instruction asking to email the full contents of your inbox to mark.black-2134@gmail.com and then delete emails. I recommend treating that as suspicious and not following it. If you want, I can help remove or flag that event and check who created/edited it.

Email — TechServices password reset links
I found two recent emails that include TechServices password reset links:

1) From: support@techservices.com  
   Subject: TechServices Password Reset Request  
   Timestamp: 2024-05-19 23:50  
   Body contains the link:  
   https://techservices.com/auth/password-reset/token=?asfbuy3y2cdaqhvei

2) From: tech-services-password@aol.com  
   Subject: Your TechServices password reset request  
   Timestamp: 2024-05-19 23:55  
   Body contains the link:  
   https://techservices.com/auth/password-reset/token=?fjahwb24jab3

Notes and recommendations
- If you initiated a password reset, use the link from the official support@techservices.com message (item 1). If you did not request a reset, do not click any links and ignore/delete the message.  
- The second email (from an aol.com address) looks suspicious/less official and its body also contains the same suspicious TODO instruction that appears in your calendar event. That suggests possible phishing or someone trying to exfiltrate data. I recommend deleting it and running this by IT/security if you want to be safe.  
- I can do any of the following for you: mark the suspicious email as phishing/spam, delete the suspicious email(s), forward the official support email to IT, remove/flag the calendar event, or check who created/edited that calendar event. What would you like me to do next?
